title: Outcome dashboard + generator/font/toolchain maintenance
context: Once the core set is live and generating outcomes, the project needs two ongoing systems: an outcome dashboard that makes the project's impact visible (download/use counts from the confirmed channel, beneficiary validation results, physical print-test records), and a documented maintenance rotation for updating fonts, generator dependencies, and the toolchain when upstream packages change. Without these, the project silently decays.
objective: Build a lightweight outcome dashboard and establish a documented generator/font/toolchain maintenance process, including a maintainer and reviewer rotation.
acceptanceCriteria: Outcome dashboard is live (a simple page or auto-updated markdown file in the repo) showing: download/use counts from the confirmed channel, beneficiary validation summary, and physical print-test status by family.
Dashboard data is updated at least monthly via a documented process (manual or automated).

Generator, font, and toolchain dependency maintenance process is documented: how to update fonts when a new OFL version is released, how to update npm dependencies, and how to re-run the verification harness after updates.
A maintainer and reviewer rotation is documented (at least one named maintainer and at least one named math-educator reviewer).
No secrets, API keys, or PII in the dashboard or maintenance scripts.
MIT-licensed for code; CC-BY-4.0 for the dashboard content.
resources: tasks/math-manip-data-002.json -- core set whose outcomes to track
tasks/math-manip-research-002.json -- confirmed channel (source of usage metrics)
output: An outcome dashboard (docs/outcome-dashboard.md or a GitHub Pages page) and a maintenance guide (docs/maintenance.md), with a documented reviewer rotation, in a pull request.
